message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Failing test: "quote for 3 runes + 3 moonstones (newcomer, two blocks) returns 137"
Current error: Expected 137, received 170 (block rule only triggers for length === 3 of one type)
Passing tests so far: 10

Per MHPCO rules: 3 runes form one block (60G base), 3 moonstones form another block (60G base) — total base 120G. Newcomer: 120 + 12 + 5 = 137.

Generalize the block rule: components (items that appear in BASE_PREMIUMS with value 25 — i.e. runes, moonstones) should be grouped by type; each group of exactly 3 alike yields 60G base; remainders priced at 25G each. Non-component items (sword/amulet/staff/potion) sum at their normal base.

Important: keep all 10 previously passing tests green. Pay special attention to:
- 3 runes block test (still 71)
- 4 runes test (still 115)
- 2 runes + 1 moonstone (still 88) — these two components are runes and one moonstone; no block, sum 75
- Single-item plain weapon tests (unaffected)

Hint: Implementation idea — separate items by whether they are components or weapons. For components, group by type and apply: floor(count/3) blocks at 60G plus (count%3) leftovers at 25G. But the spec says "block requires exactly 3" — so 4 runes counts as no block, 4×25=100. For 6 alike components a test would be needed; for now just treat exactly 3 alike as block, others as sum.

Actually — re-read the spec: "A building block of 3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G." This implies 3 alike → block; 4 → no block per the example. Be careful with 6 alike — but no test forces that. Keep minimal.

For this test: group by type; if a group has exactly 3 items, base = 60 for that group; else base = 25 × count. Sum across groups for components. Add weapon bases normally.

Run `pnpm test`.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the current implementation and make the minimal change to pass the failing test.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to handle multiple component groups:

content.text:
All 11 tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
- **Implementation**: Separated items into components (runes, moonstones) and non-components. Group components by type, then apply the block rule (60G base for exactly 3 alike) per group, summing remainders at base premium. Non-components sum normally.
- **Result**: All tests now pass (11 passing, 17 todo)
- **Approach**: Minimal generalization needed to handle the multi-block case. The block detection now operates per type-group rather than on the entire items list. Used a simple `COMPONENT_TYPES` array to identify components — this is the smallest change that handles the new test while keeping all previously passing tests green (1 rune: 33, 2 runes: 60, 3 runes block: 71, 4 runes no block: 115, 2 runes + 1 moonstone no block: 88, and all single-weapon cases unaffected).

Relevant file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_23-33-33_claim-office-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
